seo.page_title: 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G import to NZ
seo.meta_description: Import a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G to NZ with verified battery SOH, up to 385 km range and clear pricing on shipping and compliance.
seo.h1_tag: 2021 Leaf E+ G: long-range EV for NZ commutes
seo.og_title: Import a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G to New Zealand
seo.og_description: 62 kWh battery, up to 385 km range and verified battery SOH. ImportYourCar.co.nz helps NZ buyers source 2021 Leaf E+ Gs direct from Japan.
seo.primary_keyword: 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G import NZ

blocks.hero_h1: 2021 Leaf E+ G: long-range EV for NZ commutes
blocks.hero_subtitle: The 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G runs a 62 kWh battery with up to 385 km WLTP range, so most NZ commutes and weekend trips are covered without constant charging stops.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: With a 62 kWh lithium-ion battery and up to 385 km WLTP range, the 2021 Leaf E+ G comfortably covers most NZ commutes, school runs and errands on a single charge. The 160 kW motor makes motorway on-ramps and passing lanes straightforward, while CHAdeMO fast charging keeps longer trips workable when you plan your stops.

savings_block.headline: Typical savings vs NZ dealer pricing
savings_block.body: Most 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ Gs we source land in the $32,000–$42,000 range, depending on mileage, grade and options. Comparable cars on NZ yards are usually several thousand dollars higher once on-road costs are added. When we price a car, we include shipping, NZ entry certification, compliance work and our fee in the estimate so you can compare it fairly with local listings.

cta_form.headline: Find your Leaf E+ G — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Here’s a 2021 Leaf E+ G walkaround, showing the 5-seat hatch layout, 17-inch alloys and simple cabin with Apple CarPlay and Android Auto on the main screen.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G steps up from earlier Leafs with its 62 kWh lithium-ion battery (around 59 kWh usable), giving up to 385 km WLTP range when driven sensibly. A 160 kW electric motor and front-wheel drive provide brisk 0–100 km/h performance in about 7 seconds, which helps on NZ motorways and hilly routes, while strong regenerative braking works well in stop–start city traffic. ProPILOT, autonomous emergency braking and lane keep assist add useful safety support for daily commuting. Inside, you get 5-seat hatchback practicality, heated seats and Apple CarPlay/Android Auto to keep navigation and music straightforward on every trip.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: If you’re a daily commuter or small family wanting to slash fuel bills and charge at home for pennies, the 2021 Leaf E+ G is worth a close look. The 62 kWh battery and up to 385 km WLTP range help ease range anxiety for regular trips between suburbs and city areas. For the Smart Commuter who wants predictable running costs and verified battery state of health (SOH), this is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: ImportYourCar.co.nz has been helping New Zealanders bring in cars from Japan since 2009, with more than 1,000 vehicles sourced over that time. We’re an NZ-based team that focuses on clear communication, realistic pricing and explaining the process step by step. For each car we share the original Japanese auction sheet, photos and our comments so you can see what we see. Compliance, shipping and on-road preparation are treated as part of the process, not an afterthought, so you know what you’re signing up for.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives enough capacity for most NZ daily driving and regular inter-city trips without needing to charge every day.
Helps ease range anxiety for longer commutes, weekend visits and mixed city–motorway use when driven sensibly.
Delivers strong acceleration for motorway merging and hill climbs, while keeping things predictable in wet conditions.
Lets you top up quickly at compatible public chargers around NZ, making longer trips more practical with planned stops.
Adds driver assistance features that can reduce fatigue on longer drives and provide extra support in busy traffic.

cta_mid_page.subtext: Tell us your budget and mileage preferences and we’ll price real cars from the Japanese auctions for you.
cta_mid_page.button_text: Let us calculate your weekly savings.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how the 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G typically lines up when you’re weighing it against other Leaf variants and similar EVs.

rows.notes: The E+ G with the 62 kWh battery is available from 2021 onwards. For NZ, later years usually mean newer safety tech and less battery age, and all 2021 cars meet current ESC requirements for entry.

The E+ G runs a 62 kWh battery and 160 kW motor, while standard Leafs use a smaller battery and lower-output motor. In practice, the E+ G gives more range and stronger acceleration, which helps if you do regular highway or inter-city driving.

E+ G models commonly add 17-inch alloys, more equipment and the higher-capacity battery compared with base trims. Some cars have extra options like upgraded audio or additional safety packs, which we check on the auction sheet for each vehicle.

As a 2021 EV, the Leaf E+ G typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. There’s no Clean Car fee for EVs, but rules can change, so we confirm current CO2 charges or rebates and flag any entry certification risks before you commit.

2021 Leaf E+ G models appear regularly at major Japanese auctions, but good grades with low mileage attract strong bidding. That means you may wait a few weeks for the right car at the right price, especially if you’re picky about colour and options.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief, talk through battery size, range needs and budget, and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For the Leaf E+ G, we focus on battery state of health (SOH), mileage and use history, and we check any available battery health records on each shortlisted car.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet, photos and our recommendation in plain language. Once you’re happy with a specific car, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common colour and option combinations, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance, which typically passes if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Once it’s through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a Leaf E+ G is straightforward, but it helps to understand the main risks and how we handle them.

items.risk: I’m worried about battery degradation, SOH and needing an expensive battery replacement.
items.solution: For each Leaf E+ G we look for battery state of health (SOH) information, mileage patterns and use history on the auction sheet. We avoid cars with obvious red flags and talk you through what SOH means in real-world range terms before you decide.

items.risk: I’m concerned there will be hidden costs on top of the auction price.
items.solution: We break down the estimate into auction price, our fee, inland transport in Japan, shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, compliance work and on-road costs. That way you can see where each dollar goes and compare it with local dealer pricing.

items.risk: I don’t fully understand auction grades and what condition I’m actually getting.
items.solution: We explain the auction grade, interior and exterior notes in plain English and share the original documentation and photos. Based on auction documentation, this model usually presents well when you target sensible grades, but we’ll flag any scratches, repairs or wear noted before you approve a bid.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the year, budget, mileage and features you care about, plus how far you typically drive in a day. We then track suitable 2021 Leaf E+ Gs across the main Japanese auctions, filter out the weak options and send you a short list with landed pricing. If one of them looks right, we handle the bidding, shipping and NZ side while you keep control of the key decisions.

faq_block.q: What real-world range can I expect from a 2021 Leaf E+ G in NZ?
faq_block.a: The official WLTP figure is up to 385 km, but real-world range depends on battery SOH, driving style, terrain and climate. Many NZ drivers see less than the WLTP number in mixed use, so we talk through your routes and the likely range for the specific car you’re considering.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G?
faq_block.a: Most buyers land a 2021 Leaf E+ G between about $32,000 and $42,000, depending on mileage, grade and options. The total includes the auction price, our fee, inland transport in Japan, shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, compliance work and on-road costs.

faq_block.q: Will my Leaf E+ G meet NZ compliance and WoF requirements?
faq_block.a: A 2021 Leaf E+ G in standard condition typically meets NZ entry standards and can go on to get a WoF once any required work is done. We can’t promise a specific car will pass before inspection, but we use the auction sheet and photos to avoid obvious compliance risks.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving the car in NZ?
faq_block.a: From a successful bid, shipping to NZ usually takes around 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. Allow extra time for port processing, entry certification, any compliance repairs and registration, so overall you’re often looking at roughly 6–10 weeks.

faq_block.q: What auction grade should I look for on a 2021 Leaf E+ G?
faq_block.a: Many buyers aim for grades around 4 or better with a clean interior grade, but the right choice depends on budget and how fussy you are about cosmetic marks. We explain what each grade and note means, share the documentation and photos, and give our view on condition before you decide to bid.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to explore a 2021 Leaf E+ G from Japan?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us your budget, driving habits and must-have features, and we’ll come back with real 2021 Leaf E+ G options and landed pricing to compare with NZ listings.
footer_cta.button_text: Let us calculate your weekly savings.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on Leaf E+ G auction listings, EV import tips and typical landed pricing so you can move when the right car appears.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based importer that’s been sourcing vehicles from Japan since 2009. We focus on everyday cars, EVs and practical family vehicles, using direct access to Japanese auctions. Our role is to help you understand the options, costs and risks so you can decide whether importing a car is right for you.
